All right. Welcome back to your next lesson on vision. This is an absolutely crucial one. Here's the deal. One of the biggest mistakes that people make when it comes to the inner game of success is that even if you mastered all four of those core distinctions, namely, number one, the ability to manage your physiological emotional state at a very high level, two, you have empowering belief systems that support success over both the short and long term. Number three, you've harness the awesome power of vision focus as learning this module. And four, you have the highest level of standards. When it comes to making money and success, you might have all those things, but you still lack the specialized skills that you need to take that vision and make it real in the actual world. In other words, no matter what your vision is, there's going to be an outer world counterpart on the other side. In other words, do you possess the real world skills to make your vision a reality? Otherwise, nothing but a pipe dream. It's not going to happen. So in this lesson, Victoria really digs down deep and she gets to explain precisely how that process works. And so far as what are some of those specialized skills you'll need and what happens when you lack congruency? You might have the highest of standards, right? But you lack the actual skills. Now, obviously the straight line is one of those crucial linchpin skills for achieving success at anything, but it's not the only one. So let's cut to the footage right now. I'll go through that. When we're done, I'll meet you on the other side. You'll be really, really impressed. This is cool stuff. I'll see you soon. John, can you go through the specific steps that someone would need to go through to start learning this module? I'm going to go through the steps that I'm going to show you. Can you go through the specific steps that someone would need to go through to set their goals? Sure. I mean, very simple. To me, goal setting is a mathematical equation and it starts like this. Set a goal, number one, number two, put a date on it. Every goal has to have a date. Then you have short-term goals, which could be in, you know, it could be every day you could have goals, you know, end of day goals, right? You either get to make them or not make them by the end of the day and then you discard them and set new goals the next day. Those are ultra short-term goals, but you have short-term goals that are like, you know, one month goals, you have three month goals, you have intermediate goals that are like maybe a year and you have long-term goals that are three years. And then, you know, past, you know, two, three years, you start getting into sort of more to me the vision aspect of things, right? But those are the various goals. They have dates on them. Step three, so number one, set a goal. Two, put a date on it. Step three, identify the people, groups and organizations that you need to align yourself with to achieve your goal. In other words, in any goal out there, typically you're not going to do it in a vacuum. There's people you need to know, organizations you might need to join, employees you need to know how you're, whatever it might be, identify all those people, those groups, those organizations that you need to have access to. Step four, learn what you need to know. Figure out, is there stuff I got to learn? And if there is, educate yourself. Find out or hire someone that essentially knows what you need to know. So, again, let's go through it. Step one, set a goal. Step two, put a date on it. Step three, identify the people, groups and organizations that you need to align yourself with. Step four, learn what you need to know. Step five, develop a plan, meaning the best plan available based on the information you currently have. There are no perfect plans. You don't want to be one of these people that develops a plan forever. I'm still on the planning stage. When I'm done planning, then I'll get started. And there is no perfect plan because the plan that you end up developing will probably change four or five times before you actually end up getting what you want. So, but you have to have a plan. So, you develop the best plan available based on the information that you currently have about the market, about business itself and your experience. Step six, take massive action. Not a little bit of action. Take massive action towards that goal and go out and do what you got to do. Step seven, do what's called have visual acuity. Analyze your results. See what's working. See what's not working. Anytime we take actions towards a goal, we'll find that some of those actions are actually getting us what we want. And in fact, probably more of those actions in the beginning aren't getting us what we want. So, you want to actually, you know, analyze your results, see what's working, what's not, and change your approach, which is step eight is one step at a time, change your approach. So, you don't want to change everything at once, but if it's not working, you change one thing, see if it's working again, and you keep running that loop, you know, change your approach, see what's working, change your approach. How many times do you change your approach until you get what you want until you see yourself moving in the right direction? Now, there is one caveat to that is that, you know, obviously you don't want to be a moron who's bagging his head against the wall, his or her head against the wall forever. So, for a certain amount of times of change your approach, we're still not getting what you want in life. You have to say, hmm, maybe there's something wrong with the actual goal. Maybe the goal was flawed, maybe I'm missing some of the strategies myself, though, it doesn't occur in a vacuum, you know, good. After a certain point in time, you know, you don't want to get, and by the way, this is the danger of becoming emotionally invested in your goals. Because if you become so emotionally invested in your goal, and you're not achieving it, you'll become irrational, you'll irrationally be chasing after your goal, you'll keep changing your approach, and rather than saying, you know, maybe you should abandon the goals, maybe it's a bad business, maybe it's a bad idea altogether. So that's why you want to keep rational instead of becoming irrational and getting emotionally attached to the goal. So those are the steps. Let's go through one more time. Step one, set a goal. Step two, put a date on it. Step three, identify the people's groups and organizations you need to alarm yourself with. Step four, learn what you got to learn. Know what you got to know. Step five, develop a plan of attack. Step six, take massive action. Right? Step seven, analyze your results. And lastly, see what's working, what's not, change your reports. Simple as that. All right, for my money, just so you know, that's the deal. That's the power. I'm telling you, this is what causes 95% of the people in the world to fail at what they do. And I don't understand that in every business, in every endeavor, in every job function that really matters, there's going to be a specialized skill that you need to have. And if you don't have that skill, you ain't going to achieve at any reasonable level. When I was in jail, I wanted to become a world-class writer, right? I had to learn to write. What if I tried to do all this and never spent 10,000 hours painstakingly running out word, ripping up drafts, ripping up drafts? I would have never got there. Specialized skills, that's the key. So in this upcoming quiz and exercise, I'm going to make you identify what those specialized skills are in your life, in your business, and make sure we make that part of your overall plan here to ensure your success.
